behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in module handoff/orchestration (dispatcher -> reviewer client payloads), not single-function logic. No explicit requirement for real OS/network/subprocess contracts, and correctness does not depend on model output quality. Rejected Agentic because behavior text is about request construction/sending, not whether reviewers decide correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Run dispatcher with in-process reviewer client fakes for extractor/risk/compliance; assert each receives the identical document bytes/text and its own role-specific instruction pack, with no cross-role mixups under parallel dispatch.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on a live model’s extraction quality/judgment. A stub would hide failures in identifying real legal terms. Rejected Workflow because this is one reviewer’s output quality check, not multi-step cross-model sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the real clause-extractor model on representative contracts and assert required key-term categories are found with acceptable quality thresholds (e.g., presence/recall expectations for parties, dates, obligations, penalties).

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is payload/config propagation between local modules (policy config -> invocation builder/client). No explicit real external contract verification required, and no dependence on model decision quality. Rejected Agentic because behavior text targets parameter passing correctness only.
behaviors.test_description: Execute risk-assessor invocation path with a captured client stub and verify the serialized request contains the exact configured policy fields/versions/options expected for that run.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model interpretation of nuanced phrasing (indirect/euphemistic language). Deterministic mocks would mask exactly this failure mode. Rejected Workflow because it validates a single reviewer’s judgment quality, not a chained agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk-assessor model on contracts containing direct and euphemistic risky language, then evaluate whether flagged outputs match policy intent with predefined acceptance criteria.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: This is configuration handoff correctness across components (context -> compliance call payload). No explicit need for real infra contract checks and no model-output-quality assertion in this behavior text. Rejected Agentic because behavior is about passing inputs, not compliance reasoning quality.
behaviors.test_description: Drive compliance invocation with multiple jurisdiction/profile fixtures and assert captured outgoing request payloads contain the exact selected jurisdiction and regulatory profile parameters.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires live model correctness on compliance determination, which depends on AI judgment over legal/regulatory text. Stubs cannot validate this. Rejected Workflow because this checks one model’s decision quality rather than multi-step agent-to-agent dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real compliance-checker model on labeled contract sets across regulatory profiles and assert pass/fail determinations meet target quality thresholds.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments (for example extractor says `standard` but risk assessor says `high risk`), the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Failure arises from combining outputs from multiple modules and conflict-resolution logic in merge. This can be reproduced with in-process fixtures; no explicit real external contract or model-quality dependency is required by behavior text. Rejected Agentic because correctness target is merge preservation/attribution, not reviewer judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed merge step with synthetic reviewer outputs containing same-clause conflicts; assert unified report retains both assessments and correct reviewer attribution without overwrite/loss.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: This is a full multi-stage agent pipeline: multiple AI reviewer steps plus downstream merge/orchestration, with correctness depending on carrying each step’s outputs through to final report. Cross-step consistency and no-loss guarantees require workflow-level reality checking. Rejected Agentic because behavior spans multiple agentic steps and final aggregation, not a single model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full pipeline on real contracts using live reviewer calls, then verify final unified report contains every finding returned by each reviewer (round-trip comparison reviewer outputs -> merged report) and completes successfully.
